one_line_takeaway: First demonstration that MLLM visual grounding (REC) is highly vulnerable to imperceptible white-box image perturbations: untargeted attacks crash MiniGPT-v2's IoU@0.5 to ~23%, and targeted attacks can force a fixed or permuted bounding box in 30-62% of cases.
key_finding: All three attack paradigms succeed against MiniGPT-v2. Untargeted image-embedding attacks are the strongest, collapsing average IoU@0.5 from clean levels (~70-89% per split) to 23.24% average; textual bounding box attacks reduce it to 33.90% average (embedding-space disruption of visual features is more damaging than perturbing the text-generation likelihood). Exclusive targeted attacks force the fixed top-left box on ~61.84% of cases on average (vs 0.13% naturally), while permuted targeted attacks reach 30.14% average (vs 8.89% naturally). Permuted attacks are markedly harder than exclusive ones because the target boxes are smaller, position-varying, and object-specific rather than one large fixed region.
core_contribution: The paper (ICLR 2024 Workshop on Reliable and Responsible Foundation Models) is the first to study adversarial robustness of the VISUAL GROUNDING capability of multimodal LLMs (MLLMs), using referring expression comprehension (REC) as the example task. Its own contribution is three attack paradigms that craft imperceptible image perturbations against the grounding task: (1) untargeted attacks that push predicted bounding boxes away from ground truth, realized via two methods (an 'image embedding attack' that maximizes L2 distance of vision-encoder embeddings, and a 'textual bounding box attack' that minimizes the log-likelihood of the ground-truth box's textual formatting); (2) exclusive targeted attacks that force every object to be localized to one fixed target box (the top-left corner); and (3) permuted targeted attacks that rearrange boxes so each object's box is shifted to the next object (target = b_{(i+1) mod N}). Note: the MLLM grounding models it references (KOSMOS-2, Shikra, VisionLLM, MiniGPT-v2) are prior/Related Work, not this paper's contribution; the paper's novelty is the attack formulations and their evaluation.
threat_model: White-box (full-knowledge) evasion attack at inference time. The attacker knows the victim MLLM's architecture and parameters (justified by citing Bagdasaryan et al. 2023 and Qi et al. 2023) and optimizes an imperceptible additive perturbation on the input image, constrained to an L-infinity ball of magnitude epsilon = 16, using PGD (100 iterations, step size alpha = 1). The textual prompt is left unchanged; only the visual modality is perturbed. Goals are either untargeted (degrade grounding accuracy) or targeted (force a chosen/permuted bounding-box output). The authors' ethics statement restricts all experiments to the lab and disclaims real-world deployment.
limitations: Scope is narrow: a single victim model (MiniGPT-v2 7B) and a single grounding task (REC), so transferability across MLLM architectures (e.g., Shikra, KOSMOS-2) and black-box settings are untested. The threat model is fully white-box, which is the weakest attacker assumption for demonstrating real-world risk. It is a short workshop paper: no ablations on epsilon or iteration count, no proposed defense (it only frames the attacks as a 'baseline' for future robustness work), and no perceptual/qualitative human study of imperceptibility beyond the L-infinity bound of 16 (which is fairly large). The extracted abstract text in the feed record is garbled/truncated, but the full PDF was clean.

quantitative_results: Clean (no attack) IoU@0.5 ranges 70.30-89.39% across splits (e.g., RefCOCO val 84.96, test-A 89.39, test-B 82.15; RefCOCOg test 82.01)
Untargeted image embedding attack: average IoU@0.5 reduced to 23.24% (per-split lows e.g. RefCOCO+ test-B 12.64%)
Untargeted textual bounding box attack: average IoU@0.5 reduced to 33.90%
Exclusive targeted attack: average IoU@0.5 raised from 0.13% (no-attack, altered labels) to 61.84%
Permuted targeted attack: average IoU@0.5 raised from 8.89% (no-attack, altered labels) to 30.14%
Attack hyperparameters: PGD T=100 iterations, epsilon=16 (L-infinity), step size alpha=1; exclusive target = top-left box covering 4% of image area
baselines_compared: No-attack (clean image) IoU@0.5 as reference for untargeted attacks
No-attack with altered/target labels as reference for targeted (exclusive and permuted) attacks
Image embedding attack vs textual bounding box attack (the paper's two untargeted methods compared against each other)
